title: centaurea monticola subsp. orthoacantha
intro: Centaurea monticola subsp. orthoacantha is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a type of knapweed. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula, and its medicinal properties have been utilized for centuries. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Centaurea monticola subsp. orthoacantha can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
